description: Capability map for Ship Studio + Registry web scope. Ship is a package registry for agent configuration. The studio is the GUI for creating, browsing, and publishing agent packages. Registry is git-first — packages live in git repos, D1 stores metadata only.

decisions.registry_model: Git-first. Packages are git repos with .ship/ and semver tags. D1 indexes metadata. No R2 blob storage.
decisions.publishing: Git tags are source of truth. GitHub App webhooks auto-index on tag push. ship publish CLI pings index API as alternative. App not required.
decisions.unofficial_packages: @unofficial/* scope for agent configs extracted from popular public repos. Unverified status until owner claims. Migration: owner installs App + publishes → @unofficial deprecated with pointer.
decisions.dedup_strategy: Content-hash based. Skills with identical content across packages share the same SHA-256 hash. Registry surfaces content similarity without blocking duplicates.
decisions.auth_modes: Three paths: 1) anonymous — paste git URL or use studio + download files, 2) GitHub OAuth — sign in, pick repos, create PRs, 3) GitHub App install — auto-publish on tag, webhooks.
decisions.windsurf: Not supported. Scrub all references from SPEC.md, ship-studio-spec.md, EditorLogo, and any remaining code.
decisions.pro_tier: Out of scope for v0.1. Pro features (Skills IDE with CodeMirror/filesystem/testing, AI assistant) are future. Free tier = full studio + registry.

registry_index.description: D1-backed package index. Packages are git-hosted; D1 stores metadata, version history, install counts. Powers search, browse, and detail APIs.

d1_package_schema.description: Add packages table to D1 schema with drizzle
acceptance_criteria: packages table: id (nanoid PK), path (unique, e.g. 'github.com/owner/repo'), scope ('official' | 'unofficial' | 'community'), name (human display name), description, repo_url, default_branch, latest_version (semver string or null), content_hash (sha256 or null), source_type ('native' | 'imported'), claimed_by (user_id FK or null), deprecated_by (package path or null), stars (integer default 0), installs (integer default 0), indexed_at, updated_at

package_versions table: id (PK), package_id (FK), version (semver), git_tag, commit_sha (40 char), content_hash, skills_json (array of exported skill ids), agents_json (array of exported agent names), indexed_at
package_skills table: id (PK), package_id (FK), version_id (FK), skill_id (from SKILL.md frontmatter), name, description, content_hash (sha256 of skill content), content_length (bytes)
Indexes: packages(scope, installs DESC), packages(path), package_versions(package_id, indexed_at DESC), package_skills(content_hash)
Add to Repositories interface: getPackages, getPackage, upsertPackage, searchPackages, getPackageVersions, getPackageSkills, incrementInstalls
Migration SQL generated via drizzle-kit
GET /api/registry/search?q=<query>&scope=<scope>&page=<n>&limit=<n>
Searches name, description, path fields
Optional scope filter: 'official', 'unofficial', 'community', or omit for all
Returns: { packages: [{ id, path, name, description, scope, latest_version, installs, stars }], total, page }
Default limit 20, max 100
Empty query returns all packages sorted by installs DESC
No auth required — public endpoint
Path param is URL-encoded package path (e.g. 'github.com%2Fowner%2Frepo')
Returns: { package: { ...all fields }, versions: [{ version, git_tag, commit_sha, skills, agents, indexed_at }], skills: [{ skill_id, name, description, content_hash }] }
404 if package not found
No auth required
Increments installs column atomically
Returns { installs: <new count> }
No auth required (fire-and-forget from CLI or web)
Rate-limited per IP to prevent abuse (1 per package per hour)

publish_api.description: POST /api/registry/publish — index a package version from a git repo
acceptance_criteria: Body: { repo_url: string, tag?: string }
Fetches .ship/ship.toml from repo at tag (or HEAD if no tag)
Validates [module] section exists with name and version
Reads [exports] to discover published skills and agents
For each exported skill: fetches SKILL.md, extracts frontmatter, computes content_hash
Upserts package record + creates package_version + package_skills entries
If package already exists with different owner: 409 Conflict
If no .ship/ship.toml found: 422 with actionable error
Auth optional: authenticated users get package linked to their account (claimed_by). Anonymous publish creates unclaimed package.
Returns { package_id, version, skills_indexed: number }

github_webhook.description: POST /api/registry/webhook — GitHub App webhook handler for auto-publish
acceptance_criteria: Validates X-Hub-Signature-256 header against webhook secret
Handles 'create' event where ref_type='tag' — triggers publish flow
Handles 'installation' event — records App install, links to user account if signed in
Ignores all other event types with 200 OK
Tag events: extracts repo URL + tag, calls same logic as publish_api
Installation events: stores installation_id in D1 for future API calls
Returns 200 for all valid webhooks, 401 for invalid signature

unofficial_pipeline.description: Batch import pipeline for @unofficial packages. Extracts agent configs from popular public repos, indexes as unverified packages with claim path.

batch_import.description: Import agent configs from a list of popular repos and index as @unofficial packages
acceptance_criteria: Input: array of GitHub repo URLs (curated list)
For each repo: uses existing fetchRepoFiles + extractLibrary pipeline
If extractable config found: creates package with scope='unofficial', source_type='imported'
Package path: 'unofficial/<owner>-<repo>' (e.g. 'unofficial/anthropic-claude-code')
Package name: human-friendly from repo name (e.g. 'Anthropic Claude Code Agents')
Extracts individual skills from the imported library, indexes with content_hash
Skips repos that return no extractable config (no error, just skip)
Idempotent: re-running on same repo updates existing package, doesn't duplicate
Returns summary: { imported: number, skipped: number, errors: string[] }

seed_list.description: Curated list of popular repos to seed the registry
acceptance_criteria: JSON file at apps/web/src/lib/seed-repos.json
Minimum 20 repos covering: AI/ML tools, web frameworks, DevOps, languages
Each entry: { url: string, category: string }
Categories: 'ai-tools', 'web-frameworks', 'devops', 'languages', 'editors'

Include: anthropic/claude-code, vercel/next.js, facebook/react, rust-lang/rust, golang/go, microsoft/vscode, denoland/deno, oven-sh/bun, tailwindlabs/tailwindcss, sveltejs/svelte, tanstack/router, etc.
Only repos known to have CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules, AGENTS.md, or .ship/

content_hash_dedup.description: Detect content-similar skills across packages using SHA-256
acceptance_criteria: Every indexed skill has a content_hash (SHA-256 of normalized content)
Normalization: trim whitespace, normalize line endings to LF, strip YAML frontmatter for hash (hash body only)
GET /api/registry/skills/:hash/duplicates returns all packages containing a skill with that hash
Package detail API includes a 'similar_packages' field when skills share content_hash with other packages
Dedup is informational — does not block indexing

claim_flow.description: Allow repo owners to claim @unofficial packages and migrate to official
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/registry/packages/:path/claim — requires GitHub auth
Validates requester is an admin/owner of the source repo (GitHub API check)
On claim: sets claimed_by to user_id, scope remains 'unofficial' until they publish natively
When owner publishes native package (has .ship/ + tag): @unofficial package gets deprecated_by set to the native package path
Deprecated packages return a 'deprecated' field in search results with pointer to replacement
Future: ship install of deprecated package warns with migration suggestion

registry_browse_ui.description: Registry browse and package detail pages in Studio. The main discovery surface for finding and installing packages.

browse_page.description: Registry browse page with search, filters, and package grid
acceptance_criteria: Route: /studio/registry (rename templates.tsx or replace)
Search bar at top — queries /api/registry/search
Scope filter tabs: All | Official | Community | Unofficial
Package cards in grid: name, description, scope badge, latest version, install count
Unofficial packages show 'Unverified' badge in muted style
Cards link to package detail page
Pagination or infinite scroll
Empty state when no packages match
Works without auth — fully public

package_detail_page.description: Package detail page showing skills, agents, versions, and install instructions
acceptance_criteria: Route: /studio/registry/:path (URL-encoded package path)
Header: package name, description, scope badge, repo link, install count
Install command: ship add <package-path> — copyable
Skills list: name, description, content hash similarity indicator
Versions list: version, tag, date, commit SHA (truncated)
If deprecated: banner with link to replacement package
If unofficial: 'Claim this package' link (visible when authenticated as repo owner)
README section if available from repo
'Add to project' button that adds to user's studio library dependencies

dock_update.description: Update StudioDock to use 'Registry' label pointing to /studio/registry
acceptance_criteria: Registry dock item points to /studio/registry not /studio/templates
Icon remains Package or changes to Globe/Store
Templates route either removed or redirects to /studio/registry

onboarding_flow.description: Three-path onboarding: anonymous URL paste, GitHub sign-in repo picker, or blank studio. Funnels users to create their first agent package.

anonymous_url_import.description: Paste a public git repo URL to import agent config without signing in
acceptance_criteria: Input field on landing page and studio dashboard: 'Paste a GitHub URL to get started'
Accepts: https://github.com/owner/repo format
On submit: calls /api/github/import, loads result into studio library
On success: redirects to studio editor with imported config loaded
On 'no config found': shows message with option to start blank
On error: shows actionable error message
No auth required — works for any public repo
Imported library stored in localStorage (existing pattern) until user signs in

github_repo_picker.description: Authenticated flow: pick a repo from your GitHub, convert to Ship, optionally publish
acceptance_criteria: After GitHub sign-in: show repo picker (uses existing /api/github/repos)
Repos sorted by recently pushed
Each repo shows: name, description, detected config indicator (has CLAUDE.md? .cursor? .ship?)
Config detection: quick check via GitHub tree API for known file paths
Select repo → import config → load into studio editor
'Convert to Ship' button: creates PR adding .ship/ (uses existing create-pr flow)
'Add to registry' checkbox: if checked, also indexes package via publish API after PR creation
Flow: pick repo → preview imported config → edit in studio → convert (PR) → publish (optional)

download_without_account.description: Allow anonymous users to download compiled files without signing in
acceptance_criteria: Export page works without authentication
Download all button generates and downloads provider files via WASM compiler
No server call required — compilation happens client-side
Existing NoCLIState component already handles this — verify it works for anonymous users
Studio dashboard accessible without auth (currently behind ProtectedRoute — needs change)

studio_editor.description: Complete the agent editor with permissions, dependencies, and compile error surface. Make the editing experience intuitive for composing agent packages.

permissions_ui.description: Permissions editor in profile: preset picker + allow/deny/ask lists
acceptance_criteria: Permissions section in profile editor (below existing skills/MCP sections)
Preset dropdown: ship-standard, ship-guarded, ship-open, ship-plan — with one-line description of each
Allow list: text input with autocomplete for known tool names (Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep, Edit, WebFetch, Agent, mcp__*)
Deny list: same autocomplete, glob patterns supported
Ask list: same, for tools requiring confirmation
Visual: green for allow, red for deny, amber for ask
Changes compile into permissions section of CompileResult
Default: ship-standard preset if user doesn't customize

dependency_management.description: Add/view dependencies in the studio editor
acceptance_criteria: Dependencies section in studio (could be on dashboard or dedicated route)
Add dependency: text input for git URL (e.g. 'github.com/owner/repo'), optional version constraint
Calls /api/registry/search for autocomplete as user types
Installed deps list: package name, version, skill count, remove button
When a dep is added: its exported skills appear in the skills picker for the profile editor
Dep skills shown with package badge to distinguish from local skills
No server-side resolution needed — web UI adds to library's dependency list, CLI does actual resolution

compile_error_surface.description: Surface compile errors inline in the editor, not just toasts
acceptance_criteria: Permission validation errors: highlight the specific skill that requires a tool not in the allow list
Show: 'Skill <name> requires <tool> — add to permissions or remove the skill'
Error banner at top of editor (dismissible) with count of issues
Click error → scrolls to relevant section (permissions or skills)
Compile warnings (non-blocking) shown in amber
Existing toast-only errors replaced with inline error surface

publishing_wizard.description: Studio UI for publishing an agent package to the registry. Sets module identity, defines exports, and triggers indexing.

module_identity_editor.description: Set [module] fields: name, version, description, license
acceptance_criteria: Module section in studio (possibly on export/publish page)
Name field: namespaced path format (e.g. 'github.com/owner/repo') — auto-filled if repo connected
Version field: semver input with validation
Description: text area
License: dropdown with common SPDX identifiers (MIT, Apache-2.0, ISC, etc.)
These fields map to [module] section in ship.toml
Saved to user's library data in D1

exports_editor.description: Define which skills and agents are published
acceptance_criteria: Exports section shows all local skills and agents with checkboxes
Checked items are included in [exports] section
Preview of what the published package will contain
Validation: at least one export required to publish

publish_action.description: Trigger publish: indexes package in registry
acceptance_criteria: Publish button on the publish page
Requires: module name, version, at least one export
If GitHub repo connected: guides user to tag the repo, then auto-indexes via webhook
If no repo: generates .ship/ scaffold for download, shows instructions for git tag + ship publish
On success: shows package URL in registry, install command
On error: actionable message (e.g. 'Package path already claimed by another user')

windsurf_scrub.description: Remove all Windsurf references from active codebase. Not a supported provider.

remove_windsurf.description: Remove Windsurf from specs, UI components, and any remaining code
acceptance_criteria: SPEC.md: remove windsurf from provider output matrix, provider feature matrix, generated files list
ship-studio-spec.md: remove any windsurf references
EditorLogo.tsx: remove windsurf case/logo
Grep codebase for 'windsurf' (case-insensitive) outside archive/ — zero hits
No functional regressions — windsurf was not wired to anything active

sequencing_notes.phase_1: Registry backend (D1 schema + APIs) — everything else depends on this
sequencing_notes.phase_2: Seed registry (@unofficial imports) + browse UI — make the registry worth visiting
sequencing_notes.phase_3: Onboarding flow — anonymous URL import + repo picker + convert wizard
sequencing_notes.phase_4: Studio editor completeness — permissions, deps, compile errors
sequencing_notes.phase_5: Publishing wizard — close the loop: create → publish → discover
sequencing_notes.windsurf: Can run anytime in parallel, zero dependencies
